name: Cake with Fresh Fruits
recipeInstructions: About 2 hours before serving or early in the day: Prepare fruit sauce: In a 2-quart saucepan over medium heat, combine orange juice, sugar, cornstarch, orange peel, lemon peel and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and boils; boil for 1 minute. Cover the saucepan and refrigerate about 1 hour or until the mixture is chilled. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C). Line a 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 inch jelly-roll pan with waxed paper and grease it. Prepare angel food cake mix according to label directions, but spread batter evenly in prepared jelly-roll pan. Bake the cake for 20 minutes or until golden. Cover a large wire rack with a sheet of waxed paper. When the cake is done, loosen it from the sides of the pan using a small spatula and invert onto the waxed paper on the rack. Lift off the pan and peel off any paper. Cool the cake completely, then return to a clean jelly-roll pan or large tray. Cover with the cake if not using right away. To serve: Spread about half of the chilled fruit sauce over the top of the cake. Slice peaches, strawberries, and oranges. Arrange sliced peaches, strawberries, and blueberries on the cake and brush with remaining fruit sauce. Make twists of orange slices and lay around the sides of the cake. This cake is absolutely delicious and looks very pretty with the different colored fruit on top.
